name: Dick Oatts
description: American jazz saxophonist, multi-instrumentalist, composer, and educator
abstract: Richard Dennis Oatts (born April 2, 1953) is an American jazz saxophonist, multi-instrumentalist, composer, and educator.

about_quotes.speaker: Jerry Dodgion
about_quotes.evidence: Well in the saxophones there’s Frank Wess and Dick Oatts... Slide Hampton’s in the band
about_quotes.doc_title: Jerry Dodgion interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, March 9, 1996

about_quotes.speaker: Byron Stripling
about_quotes.evidence: That’s where I first met Thad Jones... So Dick said he worked with Thad Jones... He says, 'Look if you ever move to New York give me a call.'... So he flew into LaGuardia Airport... I said, 'How was that first night?'
about_quotes.doc_title: Byron Stripling interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, June 25, 2020 [interview 2 of 2]

about_quotes.speaker: Michael Abene
about_quotes.evidence: Right now Rufus Reid, Billy Hart, Ingrid Jensen is doing it, Dick Oatts on saxophone. So we’re looking to record in May.
about_quotes.doc_title: Michael Abene interviewed by Monk Rowe, Toronto, Canada, January 11, 2003

about_quotes.speaker: Tom Varner
about_quotes.evidence: There’s a fantastic alto player named Mark Taylor in Seattle who had studied with Dick Oatts, the great sax player
about_quotes.doc_title: Tom Varner interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, August 13, 2025

about_quotes.speaker: James Moody
about_quotes.evidence: You know. Dick Oat [phonetic sp.] -­ Joe Levano [indiscernible].
about_quotes.doc_title: James Moody — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
